distractor_plausibility.scoring_notes: All 15 distractors scored 3–4 (plausible misconception or strong distractor). q1: tuple/set/implicit-tuple confusions (4,4,3). q2: off-by-one and parentheses-vs-brackets (4,4,4). q3: wrong index, dict-method confusion, append-vs-modify (4,3,3). q4: swapped variables, function-call confusion, range-list confusion (4,4,3). q5: off-by-one count, wrong count, index-vs-count confusion (3,3,3).

beginner_scope.notes: All stems use plain language with HTML code tags for inline code. No acronyms introduced without explanation. No out-of-scope concepts (no list comprehensions, slicing, sorting, dicts, classes, or other un-taught constructs). All distractors are beginner misconceptions rooted in this topic (tuple vs list, wrong index, wrong method, function-call syntax, range confusion). q3 distractor marks.update(2,70) is a plausible list-method confusion for beginners; does not require dict knowledge to evaluate. q4 distractor range(scores) plausibly targets the range(len(...)) pattern taught in corpus §5.7.
